DROP TABLE IF EXISTS order_items;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS orders;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS products;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS customers;

CREATE TABLE customers (
    customer_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT,
    city TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id INT,
    order_date DATE,
    FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(customer_id)
);

CREATE TABLE products (
    product_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    product_name TEXT,
    category TEXT,
    price DECIMAL(10,2)
);

CREATE TABLE order_items (
    order_id INT,
    product_id INT,
    quantity INT,
    PRIMARY KEY (order_id, product_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES orders(order_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(product_id)
);

CREATE TABLE departments (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(50)
);

CREATE TABLE employees (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(50),
  dept_id INT REFERENCES departments(id),
  salary INT
);

INSERT INTO departments (name) VALUES
('Engineering'),
('HR');

INSERT INTO employees (name, dept_id, salary) VALUES
('Alice', 1, 6000),
('Bob', 1, 7000),
('Clara', 2, 4000);

SELECT d.name,
       COUNT(*) AS emp_count,
       ROUND(AVG(e.salary), 2) AS avg_salary
FROM departments d
JOIN employees e ON e.dept_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.name
ORDER BY avg_salary DESC;

CREATE TABLE users (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    age INT NOT NULL,
    salary NUMERIC(10,2),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

SELECT c.relname AS partition_name,
       pg_get_expr(c.relpartbound, c.oid) AS bounds
FROM pg_class c
JOIN pg_inherits i ON c.oid = i.inhrelid
JOIN pg_class p ON i.inhparent = p.oid
WHERE p.relname = 'employees'
ORDER BY c.relname;

SQLite:
CREATE TABLE departments (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE employees (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT,
  dept_id INTEGER,
  salary INTEGER
);

INSERT INTO departments VALUES
(1, 'Engineering'),
(2, 'HR');

INSERT INTO employees VALUES
(1, 'Alice', 1, 6000),
(2, 'Bob', 1, 7000),
(3, 'Clara', 2, 4000);

SELECT d.name,
       COUNT(e.id) AS emp_count,
       MAX(e.salary) AS max_salary
FROM departments d
JOIN employees e ON e.dept_id = d.id
GROUP BY d.name
ORDER BY max_salary DESC;

CREATE TABLE customers (
    customer_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    name TEXT,
    city TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id INT,
    order_date DATE,
    FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(customer_id)
);

CREATE TABLE products (
    product_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    product_name TEXT,
    category TEXT,
    price DECIMAL(10,2)
);

CREATE TABLE order_items (
    order_id INT,
    product_id INT,
    quantity INT,
    PRIMARY KEY (order_id, product_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES orders(order_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES products(product_id)
);

ALTER TABLE users RENAME TO customers;

salaries = [d['salary'] for d in db.table('users').all()]
sum(salaries) / len(salaries)

docs = db.table('users').all()
avg = sum(d['salary'] for d in docs) / len(docs)
db.table('users').search(Query().salary > avg)

table = db.table('users')
results = table.search(Query().department == 'Engineering')
sorted(results, key=lambda d: d['salary'], reverse=True)

# Update everyone in a department
db.table('users').update({'department': 'Sales'}, Query().department == 'Marketing')
